SOME/IP Protocol Specification
AUTOSAR FO Release 1.5.0
Table of Contents
1 Introduction and overview 5
1.1 Protocol purpose and objectives : 5
1.2 Applicability of the protocol : 5
1.2.1 Constraints and assumptions : 5
1.2.2 Limitations : 6
1.3 Dependencies : 6
1.4 Document Structure : 6
2 Protocol Requirements 7
2.1 Requirements Traceability : 7
3 Acronyms and Abbreviations 15
4 Protocol specification 17
4.1 Specification of SOME/IP on wire-format (Serialization) : 17
4.1.1 Header : 17
4.1.1.1 Message ID [32 Bit] : 19
4.1.1.2 Length [32 Bit] : 19
4.1.1.3 Request ID [32 Bit] : 19
4.1.1.4 Protocol Version [8 Bit] : 21
4.1.1.5 Interface Version [8 Bit] : 21
4.1.1.6 Message Type [8 Bit] : 22
4.1.1.7 Return Code [8 Bit] : 22
4.1.1.8 Payload [variable size] : 23
4.1.2 Event, Field and Eventgroup : 23
4.1.3 Endianess : 24
4.1.4 Serialization of Data Structures : 24
4.1.4.1 Basic Datatypes : 25
4.1.4.2 Structured Datatypes (structs) : 26
4.1.4.3 Structured Datatypes and Arguments with Identifier
and optional members : 27
4.1.4.4 Strings : 34
4.1.4.5 Arrays (fixed length) : 36
4.1.4.6 Dynamic Length Arrays : 37
4.1.4.7 Enumeration : 39
4.1.4.8 Bitfield : 39
4.1.4.9 Union / Variant : 39
4.2 Specification of SOME/IP Protocol : 41
4.2.1 Transport Protocol Bindings : 41
4.2.1.1 UDP Binding : 42
4.2.1.2 TCP Binding : 42
4.2.1.3 Multiple Service-Instances : 44
4.2.1.4 Transporting large SOME/IP messages of UDP
(SOME/IP-TP) : 45
3 of 66

AUTOSAR FO Release 1.5.0

4.2.3 Fire&Forget Communication : 52
4.2.4 Notification Events : 53

4.2.5 Fields : 54

4.2.6.4 Communication Errors and Handling of Communica-
tion Errors : 59

5 Configuration Parameters 63
6 Protocol usage and guidelines 64
6.1 Choosing the transport protocol : 64
6.2 Transporting CAN and FlexRay Frames : 64
6.3 Insert Padding for structs : 65
7 References 66
4 of 66
